summary:
The connective tissue width around a dental implant is primarily defined by the minimal sulcus, which measures approximately 3 mm—supported directly by multiple sources. Evidence indicates no reliable support for values ranging up to 4–4.5 mm; thus, the most accurate estimation aligns with option B (3–3.65 mm).  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to clinically grounded facts found in the provided documents.)
